title: Sergeant Exam (March 2026)
sourceNote: Imported from Word; 'Heading 1' style = correct answer (choices[0], correctIndex 0). The app shuffles choices when you take a quiz.

questions.text: In order to approve a member's timesheet row, it must be in what status?
choices: Needs Approval or Submitted
Needs Approval or New
Not Submitted or New

questions.text: You are a Homicide Detective and your teams working schedule is 0700 - 1700 hrs Monday to Friday. A homicide call came in late Monday at 1600 hours causing the team to work continuously into their next tour of duty signing off at 0800 hrs. As it relates to timekeeping, which statement is true?
choices: The “Unable to Continue” time reporting code ensures officers are paid Regular Time for up to 8 consecutive hours of their regular shift.
The final hour of the overnight shift is paid as Regular Time because that is a regular scheduled hour of work.
The officers are not permitted to return at 1600 hrs.

questions.text: You are a supervisor overseeing timekeeping for your shift. Yesterday, one of your members worked a dayshift from 0700 -1700 hrs, but was actually scheduled to work an 8 hour afternoon shift. The appropriate action is to:
choices: Conduct a shift override manually. The In / Out time reflects the appropriate 10 hours worked, but the Shift Start / Shift End needs to reflect 8 hours. This ensures that the officer gets 2 hours overtime.
Change the officers’ master schedule for that day to reflect the appropriate hours worked.
Amend the In / Out times for the date to reflect the first 8 hours worked. Add a dropdown line for an additional entry and add the remaining 2 hours to account for the officer’s overtime.

questions.text: One of your officers is scheduled to work 0600-1600 hrs, and has training from 0800-1600 hrs. What is the correct action to reflect this in timekeeping?
choices: 0600-0800 hrs 'Regular Time', and add a second line for 0800-1600 hrs 'Training'
There is no required action as the officer is 'on-duty' for the duration of the training
0600-0630 hrs 'Regular Time', leave 1.5 hrs for their lunch, and add a second line for 0800-1600 hrs 'Training'

questions.text: Sgt Brown is going on vacation for two weeks and you are tasked with filling the role with an Acting Sergeant. To capture this in e-time, you would…
choices: submit an e-transfer to Human Resources including the effective transfer and return dates, no e-time entries are required
manually change the Time Reporting Code from 'Regular Time' to 'Acting' for the dates that Sgt Brown is off
enter 'Acting for Sgt Brown' in the job code remarks for every shift being covered

questions.text: Sgt Williams is going to be off for the next 4 days and you have been tasked with filling the role with an 'Acting Sergeant'. The steps for capturing this in e-time would be to:
choices: Select the applicable Job Code for Sgt Williams to ensure member is credited with acting
submit an e-transfer to Human Resources including the effective transfer and return dates, no e- time entries are required
manually change the Time Reporting Code from 'Regular Time' to 'Acting' for the dates that Sgt Williams is off

questions.text: As a supervisor how would you get your employee to submit their vacation request?
choices: Have the employee enter their vacation time through a leave request in E-Time
Enter their vacation time manually in the timesheets
Enter their vacation time manually in the timesheet and have them enter a leave request in E- Time

questions.text: Cst Hurst has a ZBA balance of -23.50 hrs. She also has 187 hrs STAT, 4.25 hrs COMP, 17.50 hrs ACCRUED, and 8 hrs VAC remaining in her banks. The reconciliation process would be as follows:

4.25 hrs from her COMP, then 8 hrs from her VAC, and 11.25 hrs from her ACCRUED

choices: 3 calendar days

questions.text: One of the officers on your platoon advises you that she had slipped on the floor in the report room and fell. As a result she hit her head and was unconscious for a short period of time. As her supervisor you must report this incident immediately to:

questions.text: Cst Swanson broke his leg while on-duty chasing a culprit over a fence trying to effect an arrest. As their supervisor you know this needs to be reported to WSIB. What steps shall be taken?
choices: Cst Swanson shall complete a WSIB Form 6 (Worker's Report of Injury / Disease) and send it to you and HR. You shall complete a Supervisors Incident Report.
Cst Swanson shall complete a WSIB Form 7 (Worker's Report of Injury / Disease) and send it to you and HR. You shall complete a Supervisors Incident Report.
You shall complete a WSIB Form 6 (Worker's Report of Injury / Disease) and a Supervisors Incident Report and send it to HR.

questions.text: You receive a call from one of your officers during a traffic stop. She tells you that during the investigation she observed the adult driver discard a small amount of cocaine, AND the driver has no insurance. She asks you if she can write a Form 9 with both offences on it? Your response should be:
choices: No. You cannot put CDSA and provincial offences on the same release document. The CDSA charge goes to drug assignment court, and the no insurance goes to the provincial offences assignment court.
No. You need to issue a Form 9 for the drugs, and a Part III for the no insurance, but you have to use the drug assignment court dates and location for both documents.
Yes. You can issue a Form 9 for both offences, as long as you use the drug assignment court dates and the driver is over 16 years of age.

questions.text: You receive a call from one of your officers during a traffic stop. She tells you that she has a 15 year old with no licence, no insurance, and 5 ecstasy pills. She asks you if she can write three Part III Summons, one for each offence? Your response should be:
choices: No. You can issue a Form 9 - Appearance Notice for the CDSA, and a Part III Summons for each of the provincial offences, as long as you use the drug assignment court dates on all the release forms.

No. You cannot put CDSA and provincial offences on the same release document. Form 9 - Appearance Notice for the drugs goes to drug assignment court, and Part III Summons for the no license and no insurance goes to provincial offences assignment court.
Yes. You can issue a Form 9 - Appearance Notice including all three offences, as long as you use the drug assignment court dates and the driver is under 16 years of age.

Invoke the SIU Mandate by notifying the Chief of Police, SIU and Professional Standards on behalf of the Duty Inspector, who is currently unavailable.
Maintain control of the scene, notify Association representatives and isolate involved officers while determining if SIU will invoke their mandate.

questions.text: You are the Primary Investigator called to the scene of a homicide. The homicide victim is inside a dwelling house and suffering from apparent gunshot wounds. choices: Make immediate efforts enter the scene prior to written authorization and secure the phone, pending an authorized search of its contents.
Brief in the details to enable the affiant to add the phone as an item to be seized, and in the terms and conditions of the search, have the contents searched.
Re-enter the home under the authorization of the Coroners Warrant and seize the phone so it may be charged and preserved.

questions.text: You have decided to reinforce the cultural and religious considerations that should be given when searching a person in custody, to the officers on your platoon. Cst. Hunt is unsure what he should do when searching a Sikh with a turban. You advise him:

the turban is significant in the Sikh culture. A 'pat-down' search of the turban should be conducted in a dignified manner, and the turban should not be removed
considering to search a turban should be done on a case-by-case basis. The decision is ultimately up to the Staff Sergeant, and when possible be conducted by a Sikh officer.

questions.text: You have decided to reinforce the cultural and religious considerations that should be given when searching a person in custody, to the officers on your platoon. Cst. Powlinski is unsure what she should do when searching a female Muslim prisoner wearing a niqab? You advise her:

Removing a niqab is permitted to search for weapons. It must be authorized by the on-duty Staff Sergeant. It should be removed in private, by a female officer, and returned as soon as possible.
Without exigent circumstances, removing a niqab off of a Muslim female by another person is strictly prohibited. This is protected by the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms

questions.text: You have decided to reinforce the cultural and religious considerations that should be given when searching a person in custody, to the officers on your platoon. Cst. Owuso is unsure what he should do when a Muslim prisoner requests to pray while in custody. You advise him:

Muslims are required to pray three times a day. They need to remove their headdress prior to praying. They need to pray while facing west. A Qur'an may be provided if it does not compromise safety.

Muslims are required to pray multiple times a day. They prefer to pray while covering their heads as symbol of faith and respect. They need to pray while facing south. A safety blanket may be provided as a mat, if it does not compromise safety.
